You’re living on VC handouts. OpenAI and Anthropic are burning billions to subsidize your productivity. That money will run out.

When the subsidies dry up, the cost of "intelligence" will skyrocket.

The play? Build your agents and workflows now while compute is pennies on the dollar.

Lock in your advantage before the price tag catches up to the tech.

kept.text:
This is insane.🤯

Claude Code users don’t even know this exists yet.

You’re still asking
“which model codes better?”

That’s outdated.

The real move = make them compete.

OpenAI just dropped an official Codex plugin for Claude Code —
and now you can call Codex inside Claude.

This isn’t a feature.
It’s a workflow upgrade.

3 commands. All lethal:

→ /codex:review
Scans uncommitted changes or diffs.
Clean. Fast. No noise.

→ /codex:adversarial-review
This is where it gets serious.
It questions your decisions:

“Why this caching?”
“Race condition?”
“What breaks at scale?”

You can control how aggressive it gets.

→ /codex:rescue
Claude stuck?
Hand it over to Codex mid-task.
--resume continues exactly where it stopped.

And then the real weapon: Review Gate

Claude finishes → Codex auto-reviews →
Finds issues? It blocks.

No silent bugs. No guesswork.

For anything high-risk:

• Auth
• Infra scripts
• DB migrations
• Data-sensitive logic

Adversarial mode = unfair edge.

Claude writes.
Codex challenges everything.

That tension builds better code.

Stop choosing models.

Make them fight. 

(repo link in comments)
